rationale: Chandy et al. (2006) is not only listed in grouped citations; it is discussed specifically in the prior-work section as using pharmaceutical patents to characterize products. The citing paper uses that contrast to motivate its own contribution of analyzing raw ideas as originally proposed and linking them to market outcomes. It also cites Chandy et al. again in the discussion as an example of context-specific generalizability in empirical innovation research. This is a meaningful framing and positioning use, not just a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Chandy et al. (2006) as a relevant prior benchmark in invention-to-innovation research and contrasts its patent-based pharmaceutical approach with the citing paper's raw-idea and sales-outcome approach. The citation helps define what the new study adds and where its evidence sits relative to earlier work.
